segments.text: It is difficult to find short setups when the market is bullish, but there is one setup which works even very well in a bull market, and that particular setup is weak structure shot.
Weak structure chart is a setup which will work in a bullish market.
It will work in a bearish market.
It is a very specialized kind of a situation where, the stock has a very weak setup in terms of it is getting continuously sold.
And let let's have a look at this particular example to understand this.
Here, this stock is being sold continuously for last four days, four days in a row.
So what you want to see in a weak structure chart is something which is getting sold continuously.
Waterfall decline, that means there should be no positive day, just continuous selling.
And then you get a bounce right here.
There's a bounce, which was hardly a bounce.
This was just like a little pause in the stock, and this is very short, right?
And then the stock went down for another three to four days.
So it's very important to understand this particular setup, in terms of, like, kinda basically, you get a waterfall decline, you get just a small pause, and then selling continues.
Or, like, say, look at IQV.
Right?
There is a selling that just just a little bit of a pause, and the stock continues to sell.
Or let's let's look at AEHR, not here, but like in a, in this during this period.
Right?
Stock, and you can go and look at that period and see how good a setup as a weak structure shot was.
And that's the kind of setup I'm looking for, right, on a weak structure shot.
Waterfall decline, right?
The stock is down 25% here, or 36%.
It just barely manages to bounce back 2.4%.
And then here, this is very short.

So weak structure short essentially is a setup where what is happening is the whoever is holding the stock starts to liquidate the stock, or big mutual funds or big hedge funds are getting out of that stock.
And every day, they come and keep selling.
And then there is this small pause in momentum of less than three days.
And then, there are no buyers.
There are only sellers.
And again, selling comes in and you start on that particular day, and then the stock continues.
So we structure short is my favorite setup on the short side because it works in all categories, in all kind of market conditions.

And essentially, it's a very simple setup because what you're looking for is a continuous three days of continuous decline, followed by one to three days of a minor bounce of less than 2.5%, followed by selling.
And the best setups are one where the the stock just bounces one day.
So weak structure shot is something which, you know, the next section of guide, I'm gonna talk about in a little bit more detail for you to find.
